full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic cylindrical pressure hull for a 2026 collegiate AUV benchmark AUV, compliant with the 2026 collegiate AUV benchmark Team Handbook. The hull houses electronics and is rated for submerged operation to 10 m depth with a 2.5x safety factor. Geometry: cylindrical barrel 200 mm OD x 700 mm long, with two machined flanges (integral to the barrel) at each end accepting removable end caps. Each flange carries 16x M6 bolts on a 180 mm PCD. Wall thickness to be sized. The hull is rotationally symmetric, spin-formed or machined from a single billet (no welds). Material: 6061-T6 aluminum, E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. Operating conditions: external pressure at 10 m depth = 100 kPa differential; design external pressure with 2.5x factor = 250 kPa. Load cases: (R1) Operating external pressure 100 kPa; max von Mises <= yield / 3.0 = 92 MPa; ovalization / radial deflection <= 0.5 mm. (R2) Design external pressure 250 kPa (2.5x operating); max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R3) Critical buckling pressure: the hull shall buckle at >= 4.0 x operating differential = 400 kPa; linear buckling eigenvalue under 100 kPa external pressure shall be >= 4.0. (R4) End-cap bolt interface: each flange carries up to 16 x 2 kN = 32 kN axial from cap pre-load at assembly + operating differential; bolt hole bearing stress <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R5) Thruster mount boss: the hull includes 6 integral thruster mount bosses each carrying a 30 N lateral thrust; max local stress <= yield / 2.0 = 138 MPa. (R6) Hull mass <= 3.0 kg. (R7) Linear buoyancy: at operating depth with 3 kg dry + cap-plus-internals average total displaced volume must give +- 50 g trim margin (geometric check, not FEA). Primary verification: linear static + linear buckling. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required (internal electronics thermal is a separate gate). Deliverables: STEP AP242 monolithic hull, bolt pattern drawing, and a report listing hull mass in kg, max von Mises at operating and design pressures, first buckling load factor, bolt-hole bearing stress.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic cylindrical 6061-T6 aluminum pressure hull for a 2026 collegiate AUV benchmark vehicle, compliant with its 2026 Team Handbook. Hull is rotationally symmetric, spin-formed or machined from a single billet (no welds); two integral end flanges each carry 16 x M6 bolts on a 180 mm PCD for removable end caps. Rated for 10 m submerged operation with a 2.5 x design-pressure safety factor. Six integral thruster mount bosses each carry 30 N lateral thrust. Neutral-trim margin +-50 g at operating depth.

Envelope: 200 mm OD x 700 mm long barrel, wall thickness to be sized.

Load cases.
- LC1 operating external pressure: 100 kPa differential; radial ovalization <= 0.5 mm.
- LC2 design external pressure: 2.5 x operating = 250 kPa.
- LC3 buckling: linear buckling eigenvalue under 100 kPa external >= 4.0 (hull buckles at >= 400 kPa).
- LC4 end-cap bolt interface: each flange carries up to 32 kN axial (16 x 2 kN) from preload + operating differential; bolt-hole bearing check.
- LC5 thruster mount boss: 30 N lateral at each of 6 bosses, local stress check.

prompt.title: collegiate AUV benchmark 2026 10 m AUV pressure hull
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic cylindrical hull; 100 kPa operating / 250 kPa design; buckling eigenvalue >= 4.0.

boundary_conditions.mounting: axial support via internal mounting feet; end flanges sealed with caps
boundary_conditions.loads: uniform external pressure on outside; internal atmospheric

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Internal electronics thermal is a separate gate.
